reasoning: TOTP credentials are bound to local device hardware keys in the Android Keystore enclave and cannot be extracted via ADB.
remediation: Open Authenticator -> Settings -> Transfer accounts -> Export accounts. Save the displayed QR code.

reasoning: Azure AD keys and TOTP seeds are locked inside the device hardware sandbox.
remediation: Open Authenticator -> Settings -> Turn on Cloud Backup. Log in to your Microsoft Account.

reasoning: Azure AD keys and TOTP seeds are locked inside the device hardware sandbox.
remediation: Open Authenticator -> Settings -> Turn on Cloud Backup. Log in to your Microsoft Account.

reasoning: Aegis vault is encrypted locally. Standard ADB backups are disabled.
remediation: Open Aegis -> Settings -> Export -> Save password-encrypted vault to local storage and copy it to your PC.

reasoning: Chase Mobile enforces hardware device bindings. Migration will require fresh verification on the new target device.
remediation: Confirm your username, password, and security answers. Ensure you can receive verification SMS on the new phone.

reasoning: Banking profiles use cryptographic hardware bindings and prevent backup transmission.
remediation: Verify login password beforehand. Be prepared to complete SMS/Phone OTP verification on the new device.

reasoning: iMobile binds access strictly to the physical SIM card serial and device identity.
remediation: Ensure the registered SIM card is inserted into the target device before starting the iMobile activation.

reasoning: UPI configurations link accounts to SIM cards and secure enclaves.
remediation: Install Google Pay on a device containing the registered SIM card. Re-run SMS verification on sign-in.

reasoning: PhonePe UPI credentials require direct verification against the registered SIM slot identity.
remediation: Sign in on the new device with the registered SIM card active and complete SMS verification.

reasoning: UPI bindings and local logs cannot be moved. Wallet balances are server-managed.
remediation: Install Paytm, sign in with SMS verification, and enter UPI transaction codes.

reasoning: Signal databases use local SQLCipher encryption, and Signal explicitly disables standard Android backup capabilities.
remediation: Open Signal -> Profile -> Chats -> Chat backups -> Turn on. Write down the 30-digit passphrase and copy the backup file from /sdcard/Signal/Backups/ to your PC.

reasoning: WhatsApp disables standard ADB backup. Database decryption keys reside in root-protected private sandboxes.
remediation: Open WhatsApp -> Settings -> Chats -> Chat backup -> Tap Backup to Google Drive. Or copy the media folder /sdcard/Android/media/com.whatsapp/ to your PC.

reasoning: Telegram cloud chats sync automatically. Local-only Secret Chats cannot be backed up and will be lost.
remediation: Secret Chats cannot be restored. Standard cloud chats will sync automatically once verified via SMS on the new device.

reasoning: Profile feed data is cloud-backed. Direct login state must be authenticated.
remediation: Verify that your account credentials and 2FA settings are active on another device.

reasoning: Instagram accounts sync to servers. Offline draft stories and posts are lost on reset.
remediation: Publish or delete pending media drafts before wiping your old device.

reasoning: Unpublished tweets and message drafts reside only on local sandboxes.
remediation: Publish or save twitter draft notes before wiping the device.

reasoning: Email databases reside on Google Cloud servers and sync automatically.
remediation: Ensure Google Account Sync is active inside Android Account Settings.

reasoning: Mailbox accounts and calendars are server-managed.
remediation: Re-add email addresses and credentials manually in the new Outlook app.

reasoning: Local vault caches are encrypted. Forgetting Master Passwords or losing offline entries will cause lockout.
remediation: Verify that your local vault is synchronized with the Bitwarden cloud server before wiping. Know your Master Password.

reasoning: Master key vaults sync to the cloud. Secret keys are required for sign-ins.
remediation: Locate your 1Password Emergency Kit containing your Secret Key before performing resets.

reasoning: KeePassDX stores vaults entirely locally in a .kdbx file.
remediation: Copy your password vault .kdbx file off the phone onto your PC via USB before resetting.

reasoning: All files sync to the Google Drive cloud workspace.
remediation: Ensure any files checked as 'available offline' have finished syncing changes.

reasoning: Files reside on cloud servers. Cache is rebuilt on login.
remediation: Check for any active uploading indicators in the status panel before resetting.

reasoning: Storage is server-managed and matches corporate directories.
remediation: Log in using credentials to re-sync folders on the new device.

reasoning: Obsidian stores notes as raw local files on the device filesystem. Obsidian does not sync notes automatically to the cloud for free.
remediation: Connect your phone to your PC and manually copy your notes vault folder (typically under /sdcard/Documents/Obsidian/) to your computer.

reasoning: Notes and checklists sync automatically in real-time to Google servers.
remediation: Verify Google Keep Sync is enabled under Google Settings -> Sync options.

reasoning: Notebooks sync to OneDrive storage accounts.
remediation: Ensure notebooks show a fully synchronized state in the OneNote menu.

reasoning: Unsynced local media will be wiped. Cloud backups are standard.
remediation: Confirm in-app status reads 'Backup complete' or copy '/sdcard/DCIM/' to a PC.

reasoning: No cloud backup agent exists in this application.
remediation: Manually copy your media directories (DCIM/Pictures) to your computer.

reasoning: VPN profile settings sync from servers.
remediation: Retrieve your ExpressVPN login details or activation key before device wipe.

reasoning: Profiles and server lists are cloud-backed.
remediation: Install the app and log in on the target device.

reasoning: Boards and task lists are hosted on Trello cloud systems.
remediation: Sign in on the new device to automatically restore access.

reasoning: Workspace conversations reside on Slack cloud systems.
remediation: Know workspace URLs and login details before resetting.

reasoning: Corporate channels are hosted on Microsoft 365 servers.
remediation: Log in with enterprise credentials. Solve corporate MFA checks on the new device.
